summary: Closed, unmerged PR that decompiled five small stubs across gmregclear, grcastle, groldpupupu, and tydisplay while also adding substantial Claude/overnight automation docs and scripts. The C/header changes removed stub markers, added typed prototypes, exposed one gmregclear struct field, and the PR body claims `ninja`, `ninja diff`, and 100% objdiff matches. The only recorded feedback is a decomp.dev GALE01 bot report saying "No changes"; no human review explains why the PR was closed without merge.

key_files.role: Decompiled `fn_8017DE54` and `gm_80180BA0`; split `lbl_80472E48_t` padding to expose `x14[0x1B]` at offset 0x14.
Updated prototypes from `UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS` to `s32 fn_8017DE54(u8, u8*)` and `void gm_80180BA0(void)`.
Decompiled `fn_801CFAFC`, including a castle ground field write and conditional ftLib call.
Added `<melee/it/forward.h>` and typed `fn_801CFAFC(Item_GObj*, Ground*, Vec3*, HSD_GObj*, f32)`; also changed `fn_801CFB68` back to `UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS` with no explanation in the slice.
Decompiled `un_8031BB34` as an index-to-`ToyDsp..._Top_joint` string-table lookup with `idx == -1` normalized to 0.
New Claude Code/decompilation guide covering build commands, style, naming, matching tactics, objdiff, and permuter notes.
New autonomous overnight decomp runner using stubs, GitHub conflict checks, worktrees, Claude sessions, build/match checks, retries, logs, and optional PR creation.
New helper subcommands for progress display, stream-json parsing, token usage, stub filtering, context trimming, asm extraction, progress tracking, and rate-limit handling.
New JSON stub scanner using `/// #function` markers and `config/GALE01/symbols.txt` sizes.
Wrapper to scan all `src/melee/*.c` files containing stub markers.
New macOS launchd scheduler for the overnight decomp runner.

what_changed: Replaced five `/// #...` stubs with C implementations: `gm_80180BA0`, `fn_8017DE54`, `grOldPupupu_80211C1C`, `fn_801CFAFC`, and `un_8031BB34`.
`fn_8017DE54(u8 arg0, u8* arg1)` now returns counts based on flag bits `0x20`, `0x10`, and `2`, comparing up to three `arg1` bytes against `0x21`.
`gm_80180BA0` loops over 0x1B entries, uses `gm_80164024`, and writes `lbl_80472E48.x14[idx] * 0xA` through `gmMainLib_8015D06C`.

`grOldPupupu_80211C1C` retrieves another ground object via `Ground_801C2BA4(7)`, checks `other_gp->gv.unk.xC8 == 2` and `xD4 != 0`, then calls `grAnime_801C8138` using `grOp_804D48E0[other_gp->gv.unk.xCC][other_gp->gv.unk.xD8]`.
`fn_801CFAFC` writes `4` as an `s16` through `&gp->gv.castle2.xC4` and calls `ftLib_80086A4C(gobj, (f32) grCs_804D6970->x4)` when `ftLib_80086960(gobj)` is true.
`un_8031BB34(s8 idx)` builds a local table of trophy display joint-name strings such as `ToyDspQues_Top_joint`, maps `idx == -1` to 0, and returns the selected pointer cast to `s32`.
Header updates removed unknown prototypes for the newly implemented gm/gr functions and added the missing item forward declaration needed by `Item_GObj*`.
Large unadvertised tooling/docs additions introduced `CLAUDE.md`, an autonomous decomp runner, helper commands, stub scanners, a launchd scheduler, and ignored `scripts/logs/`.
smart_moves: Exposed `lbl_80472E48_t.x14[0x1B]` by splitting padding instead of indexing into a raw byte blob, while keeping the existing `STATIC_ASSERT(sizeof(...) == 0x80)` guard.
Updated prototypes and includes alongside decompiled bodies so callers and headers reflect real return/argument types instead of `UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS` for the new functions.
Used `PAD_STACK(8)` in `grOldPupupu_80211C1C`, a common matching tactic when code is logically correct but frame size is short.
Kept narrow casts and explicit types in matching-sensitive code: `(u8) i`, `(s32) arg1[...]`, `(f32) grCs_804D6970->x4`, and an `s16` write to the castle field.

The automation scripts had practical safeguards: require a clean worktree, filter functions mentioned in open PRs/issues, avoid already-tried functions, batch small same-file stubs, and build only the target object in Claude worktrees.
`decomp_helpers.py` parsed Claude stream JSON result events instead of relying only on grep over conversational text, which is a more robust automation pattern.
Verification was explicitly recorded in the PR body: `ninja`, `ninja diff`, and all functions 100% in objdiff; the decomp.dev bot also reported GALE01 "No changes".
decomp_lessons: When assembly indexes a previously padded global, split the pad into a typed field at the observed offset and preserve the struct size with `STATIC_ASSERT`.
For small stage callbacks, compare stack frames; a single `PAD_STACK(8)` can be the difference between a semantic match and a binary match.
Typed prototypes should accompany stub removals, and missing forward headers should be added at the header level when a signature introduces types like `Item_GObj*`.
If a raw field cast such as `*(s16*) &gp->gv.castle2.xC4` is needed to match, consider a later struct/type cleanup so future work can avoid raw offset casting.
Local pointer/string tables can be a valid way to match lookup functions like `un_8031BB34`; handle signed sentinel indices before indexing.

The slice gives no closure reason, but this PR mixed 5-function decomp work with over 1,200 lines of local automation/docs. Future work should strongly consider splitting matching C/header changes from agent tooling unless maintainers explicitly request both.

Changing a neighboring function prototype from typed to `UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS` should be documented or avoided; `grcastle.h` did this for `fn_801CFB68` even though the slice shows an existing typed C definition.
naming_conventions: The PR title uses subsystem prefixes `gm/gr/ty` for game-mode, ground/stage, and trophy work.
Implemented symbols keep existing project/address names: `gm_80180BA0`, `fn_8017DE54`, `grOldPupupu_80211C1C`, `fn_801CFAFC`, and `un_8031BB34`; no new descriptive renames were introduced.
New static stage data follows the local stage prefix style: `grOp_804D48E0` in `groldpupupu.c`.
The new gmregclear struct member uses the offset-derived unknown-field style `x14` after `pad_C`.

Added `CLAUDE.md` states broader project conventions: file names lowerCamelCase, functions with module/file prefixes and UpperCamelCase examples, module prefixes like `ft`, `it`, `gr`, `gm`, `lb`, and struct members annotated with hex offsets.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: `gm_80180BA0` keeps the submitted call pattern with `gm_80164024((u8) i)` assigned to `idx` and called again for `gmMainLib_8015D06C`; the diff does not explain whether this was for regalloc/call-order matching.
`fn_8017DE54` uses a pointer temp `p = &arg1[1]` plus explicit `(s32)` casts before byte comparisons to `0x21`.
`fn_801CFAFC` uses an `s16` lvalue cast on a ground union field and an explicit `(f32)` cast for the ftLib argument.
`un_8031BB34` represents the lookup as a stack/local `char* table[]` of fixed string literals and returns the pointer as `s32`.

The added automation prompt in `decomp_overnight.sh` encodes matching tactics for future agents: use nearby source style, verify enum numeric values, check bitfield LSB order, compare stack frame sizes for `PAD_STACK`, build the target object only, and inspect objdiff/report data.
review_feedback: No human review comments and no review records are present in the slice.
The only issue comment is from `decomp-dev[bot]`: `Report for GALE01 (8446da7 - d93e838) No changes`.
The PR was closed about three minutes after creation and was not merged; the slice does not state why.
follow_up_queries: Why was PR #2278 closed without merge despite the bot reporting no GALE01 changes?
Were `CLAUDE.md` and the `scripts/decomp_overnight*` automation files intended for the main repo, or should they remain out-of-tree/local?
If resurrecting the decomp work, split the five C/header matches from the automation/docs changes into separate PRs.
Audit `src/melee/gr/grcastle.h`: should `fn_801CFB68` remain typed instead of being changed back to `UNK_RET(UNK_PARAMS)`?
Can `gp->gv.castle2.xC4` be modeled as an `s16` field to remove the raw cast in `fn_801CFAFC`?
Validate the data shape and linkage for `grOp_804D48E0[1][2] = {{0, 0}}` against the original data section before reusing.
